programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky isn't happy about Pat going away. Nigel is upset when Debbie works late. Sharon offers Peggy the licence

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Phil and Sam are sent to Romania in search of a missing woman thought to be the victim of a ruthless people-trafficking operation. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Linden returns to Holby to treat the expectant mother of conjoined twins, even though he is still officially suspended. Staff arrange a surprise engagement party for Faye and Joseph, while Maddy becomes suspicious of Jamie's involvement in the death of Liam Harris

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack tries to persuade a reluctant Tony to accept Eddie's offer of a kidney and Simon is shocked when Jane leaves hospital early, especially as she refuses to let him care for her. Duffy faces a life-changing decision following Ryan's return to Holby and Anna is devastated to learn the truth about Merlin. Meanwhile, Les Dennis makes a guest appearance as a bullying boss who drives an employee to suicide and finds his life in danger from her enraged husband

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Phil and Sam are sent to Romania in search of a missing woman thought to be the victim of a ruthless people-trafficking operation. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky isn't happy about Pat going away. Nigel is upset when Debbie works late. Sharon offers Peggy the licence

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The Highland GP has a run-in with an old friend and later investigates what appears to be an attack on a gay man, only to find himself caught up in a traumatic domestic violence case. David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dawn rushes to be by Andrew's side as he hovers near death, while Sam is tormented by the thought that he could have done more to help his stricken colleague. Joanna wrestles with a medical dilemma as her teenage patient recoils from the prospect of undergoing the risky operation to remove her brain tumour. Starring Gary Mavers, Joseph Millson and Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Dalbys' long spell of misfortune comes to an end as James stumbles on the cause of the disease afflicting the family's herd. Mrs Hall advises James to get on with it vis-a-vis Helen and warns him that he risks losing her, with Richard Edmundson still vying for her affections. James finally pops the question, but shudders at the thought of having to ask Mr Alderson's permission

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester is frustrated by William's snoring, and a neighbour's pet adds to her problems. Comedy, guest starring Stephanie Cole, with Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The ladies bowling team nickname Tom 'Superstud' when Diana ends up in his bed after the residents are bequeathed £200 to get drunk at a wake. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene decides to run for the Spanish border but is stopped before he can make it across - and General Von Klinkerhoffen gets hold of the painting. The British airmen become trapped in wine barrels in the cellar of the chateau

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The ladies of the village feel the need for a little excitement and hire a minibus to take them out for a picnic. However, against all the odds, their trip turns into a mystery tour. Julie T Wallace and Trevor Bannister guest star

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unpopular colonel is found dead in the vicarage of St Mary Mead and investigations reveal no shortage of suspects - including the vicar and members of the victim's family. Christmas edition from 1986, starring Joan Hickson, Paul Eddington and James Hazeldine

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team unearth the 10-year-old case of a poet from Belfast whose burnt body was found in the scrapyard of a known gangster. Gerry becomes convinced the victim's links with the criminal fraternity led to his demise, but Brian has a different theory, believing the secret of his murder lies in his verse. Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Alun Armstrong star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Zoe is put on trial for conspiracy to murder following an abortive mission in which an undercover police officer was killed by a gang of Turkish gun-runners. Espionage drama, guest starring Ian McNeice alongside Keeley Hawes and David Oyelowo

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: American diplomat Ryan Reed is shot dead and the team arrives at the scene to find his body posed - sitting upright on a bench with his hand tucked inside his jacket. An argument quickly ensues between the police and the US embassy, which claims the murder falls under its jurisdiction, while the Lyell crew fights against the bureaucracy of the FBI, aided only by the US deputy chief of mission Matt Garcia. However, when he goes missing, Nikki is concerned he might be the next target

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. However, he returns with a houseful of distinguished guests, including the beautiful Blanche Ingram and her opinionated mother. Things turn sour when a mysterious visitor arrives. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square and its residents celebrate VE-Day, except Grant who goes to a private reunion, and Ethel and Dr Legg who prefer to remember those who died at the new memorial

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Phil and Sam are sent to Romania in search of a missing woman thought to be the victim of a ruthless people-trafficking operation. programme.title: Holby City

Maddy enlists Sam's help in another effort to prove Linden is innocent, and staff come under pressure thanks to a Home Office inspection connected to the North Korean couple who are expecting conjoined twins

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: New paramedic Luke Warren is thrown in at the deep end when he is trapped in the cellar of a house destroyed by a gas explosion and back at Holby City he also receives plenty of female attention _ but his ex-wife Bex, makes it clear why they are no longer married. Jack returns to work as janitor but is understandably worried on the day of Tony's transplant operation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The son of an ex-con under Terry's supervision is arrested for involvement in a protection racket, but is later found beaten semi-conscious by a gang of heavies. Yvonne helps a homeless man being harassed by teenagers, but when she turns a car wash power jet on one of the offenders her unprofessional conduct is caught on camera, putting her job on the line. Roger reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy drops the twins off at nursery on their first day. Ricky makes up with Pat, David gives Bianca a driving lesson. Sharon tells Michelle she's selling up and leaving forever

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square and its residents celebrate VE-Day, except Grant who goes to a private reunion, and Ethel and Dr Legg who prefer to remember those who died at the new memorial

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Highland medic Dr Finlay begins to suspect the Reverend Gill may be more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next, and sets out to investigate. Meanwhile, his colleague Dr Napier delivers glad tidings to a married woman - but does not receive the joyful response he anticipated. Rural drama, with David Rintoul and Jessica Turner

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Sam tries to help an overweight local woman whose efforts to slim are placing her life in jeopardy, but only ends up making matters worse, while one of Joanna's patients sparks off yet another major health scare in Cardale. Rural medical drama, starring Joseph Millson and Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James addresses the misconceptions of an awkward farmer who prefers to treat his own animals, and Siegfried faces a dilemma as James and Helen's honeymoon looms. A chance encounter with old friend Stewie Brannon ruins Siegfried's chances of landing a plum job with the county horse racing board

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides the community needs an old folks' home, and takes her fight to the local council. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana decide they would like a holiday, but face the task of upsetting the devious Harvey's construction plans to raise the necessary cash. Comedy, with Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: General Von Klinkerhoffen orders Edith to marry Captain Bertorelli as a propaganda exercise, while the resistance tries to rescue the airmen who are trapped in wine barrels in the chateau's cellar. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Carmen Silvera and Vicki Michelle

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley, making good use of his engineering background, designs a mobile vending machine suitable for the thirsty and foolhardy. However, when he takes his device to the golf course, it causes more embarrassment for a beleaguered Barry. Gordon Wharmby stars

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: Kavanagh defends a left-wing activist accused of murdering someone from the opposite end of the political spectrum - and learns a harsh lesson in the process. To make matters worse, he finds out daughter Kate is having an affair with her married tutor, thus setting the scene for further fireworks. John Thaw stars

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of an East German immigrant, but all they have to go on are his mysterious final words, which translate as 'blue flower'. As they piece together his remarkable story, Sandra tries to gain the trust of his estranged daughter. Crime drama, starring Amanda Redman

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents deal with a wave of computer crime which has seen the ingredients of painkillers tampered with, bank balances erased and traffic lights disrupted to devastating effect. Ruth believes it may be the work of an Islamic terrorist faction and enlists the aid of the National Hi-Tech Crime Unit to uncover the truth. Meanwhile, Danny comes under pressure to reveal what has happened to Zoe. Espionage drama, starring Nicola Walker

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki and Jack attend the scene of a fatal car crash in which a driver lost control of her vehicle and the forensic evidence suggests it may not have been an accident and that the victim's son Kevin McDowd - a resident of Kingscote Bows care home - could be involved in the death. The teams begin to notice a pattern of foul play and a connection between the care home and other suspicious deaths that had previously gone unnoticed, while Kevin absconds and takes his friend Serena with him

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides the community needs an old folks' home, and takes her fight to the local council. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie

programme.desc: Lydia gets a shock when Delia and Binnie give her a reference, and Robbie gets a dose of reality when Carol examines his job prospects

Maddy finally discovers that Linden was not responsible for Liam Harris's death - and Mark has his hands full playing the role of peacekeeper when Lola and Faye have problems working together

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lara's nerve is tested when she is confronted by a life-threatening situation, and in the ensuing commotion Harry and Tally patch up their problems. Merlin is devastated by a revelation from Anna, while Eddie develops post-operative septicaemia and a distraught Jack has to face the possibility that his father might die

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max Little is pushed to the limit by his ex-wife's involvement with big-time gangster Barry Green, and Leela bonds with a busker who has been found beaten and robbed in the park, but faces a dilemma when he turns out to be an illegal immigrant. Heaton informs Yvonne her suspension will be dropped if she curbs her creative approach to policing, while Terry discovers he has a second chance with ex-wife Helen

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mr Soames chases people for their rent, Nigel stops the football team drinking, and Ian makes a disastrous birthday purchase for Cindy - twice

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Lydia gets a shock when Delia and Binnie give her a reference, and Robbie gets a dose of reality when Carol examines his job prospects

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: A distraught Dr Napier berates herself for failing to diagnose a case of diphtheria, while Dr Cameron shoulders the melancholy task of attending an old friend who is hovering at death's door. Drama, starring David Rintoul

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Joanna is presented with an unenviable dilemma by old flame Martin Henderson, who asks her to leave Cardale and start a new life with him. Meanwhile, an elderly lady reluctant to seek help gives Sam cause for concern. Medical drama, starring Haydn Gwynne, Christian Burgess and Joseph Millson

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Helen are on their honeymoon and start the tuberculin testing, while Siegfried helps Tristan prepare for his final exams in Edinburgh

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: The Antiques Roadshow comes to town, so William and Hester take along their Chinese vase to get a valuation. On their arrival back home the pair surprise a burglar, so Hester tries out her newly acquired martial arts skills. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Diana is persuaded to broaden her horizons, so she ventures into politics and decides to stand as a candidate for the Residents' Party. Unfortunately, Tom and Harvey ruin her plans by running against her. Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The British airmen get stuck down the drain in the village square, and Rene plans to rescue them using an old ice-cream van. As a result, General Von Klinkerhoffen gets more than he bargained for when he decides to buy one of the cool refreshments on sale. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy Hardcastle, also known as the Hawk, claims to be a descendant of Robin Hood - but doesn't share his illustrious ancestor's flair for adventure. Peter Sallis and Keith Clifford star

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: An old friend asks the detective to investigate the death of his father, which he is convinced should be treated as murder. Due to his suspension from the police force, Wallander refuses to get involved - but finds himself drawn back to the case when his acquaintance is also found dead. Drama, starring Kenneth Branagh and Tom Hiddleston, with Rupert Graves, Angela Thorne and Vincent Regan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A rock star and his supermodel wife, whose careers are dwindling, report the kidnapping of their baby. Adam is assigned to investigate, but soon discovers the parents are not telling him the whole truth and may even have engineered the situation to boost their flagging stardom. Andy Serkis, who played Gollum in The Lord of the Rings films, guest stars as the desperate musician, alongside Arabella Weir and Tim McInnerny

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A clergyman and his two sisters take a heartbroken Jane under their wing after finding her alone and starving on the moors. The governess embarks on a new life as a parish teacher, but thinks she hears Rochester call out her name and feels compelled to return to Thornfield - where the secrets of the North Tower are finally revealed. Starring Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a Victorian railway lamp, a musical jewellery box and a Brownie toadstool

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Thompson tells Callum they are not getting enough from the listening device and suggests that he should start wearing a wire - much to his horror. Whitney tells Gray he should be with his children for the funeral even though she is terrified of facing court without him. Secrets fester between Mick and Linda, and Ash is horrified by what Suki has done

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers visit a London bar with unpaid debts concerning work on the air conditioning, and a classic car dealer - but the man they meet at the car lot claims to have just taken over the premises. Plus, the case of a cleaner who took her employer to an employment tribunal over unpaid sick and holiday pay

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Three weeks on, the residents each face decisions about their future. Gail tries to remain dedicated to her marriage, while Belle finds raising Maya has an impact on her love life. David meets Kelly's university friends, and is left uncertain how well he knows her, while a chance encounter sees him reunited with Saira. Andy has a tense altercation with Liam that forces him to consider what he really wants out of life

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Simi and Prescillia test their commitment before deciding whether to move in together

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Phoebe and Ruairi test whether they are strong enough to maintain a relationship between two countries as Phoebe looks to study abroad

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the third episode, crew members in the capital respond to reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Elsewhere, Adam and Orani are dispatched to a long-term heroin user whose concerned mum has called for help

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: A report on criminals posing as key workers who played on people's fear to extract money from them, and scams involving hot tubs that have left consumers out of hundreds of pounds

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Langley Mill in Derbyshire, Tylorstown in Rhondda Cynon Taf, and Milton Regis in Kent

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A police officer shot at close range is in danger of losing his sight, and a speeding motorcyclist is pursued through London

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An air ambulance is sent out to rescue a deer manager in the Highlands who has been crushed by his own quad bike, and paramedics treats an elderly man who has fallen

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Jonathan Pratt give advice to two teams at Wetherby racecourse. Plus, host Charlie Ross heads to the Royal Armouries in Leeds to learn about animal armour

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem has problems rounding up the villagers for their vaccinations, while the Rev Todd is convinced that only he has the power to save the village. Todd learns about his daughter's affair with a married man and decides to punish her. Comedy drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar and Mark Heap

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history behind some of the country's most popular spices and explore how they have been used to liven up meals. The culinary duo also visit a chilli festival - with mouth-numbing consequences

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton visits rural Shropshire with a couple who want their £500,000 budget to afford them and their young children a life in the outdoors with community close by. While in the county, Alistair visits the mansion of Attingham Park to see the recent restoration successes for himself, as well as discovering some intriguing stories from its past

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in York, helping a couple who have spent two years painstakingly renovating their terraced home. With a very small garden full of crazy paving and building materials, Charlie and the boys compete to give it a £5,000 facelift, turning it into a luxurious haven to relax and entertain in. Along the way, they have a host of ideas and tips for making the most of small spaces and shady corners

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a pocket-sized racing car, a century-old weather vane, and a much loved but horribly damaged leather rhinoceros

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Quiz, presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich Brothers are asked to tackle a front garden in Ilkley, with owners Denise and John having to choose designs based on cottage gardens

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that once belonged to a man's grandfather, who served as a piper and a soldier in the Royal Scots during the First World War. Elsewhere, silversmith Brenton West helps to restore a Welsh crown of cultural significance that bears little resemblance to its former splendid self, clockwork genius Steve Fletcher takes on the repair of a quirky tortoise-shaped bell, and furniture restorer Will Kirk is thrilled to be presented with an enormous challenge - an antique wooden chair dating from the Carolean period

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Thirty minutes into the night shift, police request urgent assistance at the scene of an incident - a man has called to say his mum has been stabbed. On arrival paramedics find she is in cardiac arrest, and she is declared the sickest patient in London by Ambulance Control. Across town, Josh and Vinny are dealing with more practical issues. They've been dispatched to their first patient of the night - a woman who's collapsed in a casino. But to reach her, they need to first navigate Leicester Square, which is currently teeming with revellers. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Darby runs into an old flame and spends the rest of the day trying to get him off her mind. Romantic comedy anthology series, starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Feeling empowered after her experience with Danny, Darby embarks on a non-committal hook-up bender - until she falls for up-and-coming chef Magnus

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Checking into the Shankly this week is a party of 41 stags, who are being looked after by trainee guest relations manager James. But the morning before the lads arrive, he is nowhere to be found. Owner Lawrence challenges staff to set up a glamping festival on the roof in just one week, a loved-up couple need a last-minute bridal suite and a bride-to-be brings her friends to the hotel for her second hen party this year

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Louise Minchin and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela meets fed-up pedestrians who want to bring an end to motorists parking on the pavement. They say it is dangerous and inconsiderate - drivers say they have no choice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander follow the progress of properties in the London borough of Camden, Coatbridge in North Lanarkshire and Bootle on Merseyside

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A former policeman in Northern Ireland forced to rebuild his life after a rocket attack on his vehicle and a Leeds paramedic threatened by a hospital patient with a 12-inch knife

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An accident on the Isle of Mull leaves a man seriously injured, triggering a response from the air ambulance. A tourist in Edinburgh has breathing problems

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Raj Bisram help two teams scour an antiques fair in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp sets a challenge for some other people attending

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The quarantine continues and the supplies start getting low, and Prem is no wiser about the source of the virus. Megan and Emlyn come up with a plan to get more food from the next village, but events take a turn when Mary arrives with terrible news

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the British tradition of cooking with food hunted from the land and prepare a selection of dishes using rabbit, grouse and venison

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking for a property in Gloucestershire on a budget of £850,000 and visits an ice cream-making family on Rodburgh Common

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a shattered ceramic dish, a hand-made doll's house and a vintage harmonium that provided the soundtrack to many an East End knees-up

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Chantelle's funeral arrives, and as family and friends gather to say goodbye, Gray struggles to hold it together. Whitney is about to face her first day in court for the death of Leo, with Sonia there for support - but neither of them are prepared for what happens next. Phil visits Raymond in hospital, feeling an instant bond with the boy, while Jean warns Kush about the risks of online gambling

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: A busy Friday sees almost 700 party-mad people checking in. But with just minutes to go, there are problems with the plumbing. It's even worse for Charley, who has arrived for a hen party organised by her brother, as there are problems with their room - and general manager Damian struggles to placate them. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present the weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: An unusual piece of sporting history arrives for the attention of silversmith Brenton West and leather expert Suzie Fletcher - a decorative belt awarded to a Victorian speed walker. A 1950s metal mobo roundabout and a dented trumpet are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A rural village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight and a firearms team fear for their lives after being confronted by an armed man

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost celebrates some of the glorious gardens the programme has visited over the last few years and gives plenty of seasonal hints and tips for viewers to be doing in our own gardens. Frances Tophill visits a lochside retreat, Joe Swift finds lots of take home tips from a city garden, Carol Klein is inspired by plant combinations in West Yorkshire and Advolly Richmond shows how a historical landscape was designed for fun and frolic. Plus, the couple who let nature be their gardening guide and a gardener who makes containers her focus of attention

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Three people who have never met before must work as a team and answer questions correctly in a bid to win a share of a £20,000 cash prize. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Exeter, former air traffic controller Paul Johnson takes on behavioural safety consultant Keith Jackson in the antiques challenge. Along the way presenter Paul Martin teaches them the tricks of the trade and the dos and don'ts of how to be an antique dealer

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: A statue of Weld's celebrated founder has been desecrated and Constable Tom challenges George to go one better and solve the mystery of who did it

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The quarter-final sees the five remaining contestants tackle three activewear challenges, beginning by following a pattern to make a man's Lycra cycling top, which involves a complex zip insertion. Next, they transform garish 1980s ski suits into children's jackets, before they end by creating yoga outfits - the most fitted garments ever attempted on the programme. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young then decide which one leaves the competition and misses out on the semi-final. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to the Democratic Republic of Congo in the hope of finding the gorilla family again, and discovering the outcome of silverback Chimanuka's confrontation with a rival. He soon finds the apes, but discovers their lives are now under threat from poachers, and works with the local rangers to prevent them coming to harm

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Compilation edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series and offering some new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits Rodmarton Manor, an extraordinary Arts and Crafts house in the Cotswolds

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: The trawler Reliance is halfway through a fishing trip off the Shetland Isles when skipper John Clarke spots a broken rope that could lead to the boat losing its net. The crew fix the problem and put out the nets again, but they are hit by a rogue wave that rips a two-ton metal bar off the top of the boat. In the remote Norwegian sector of the north sea, the trawler Rosebloom is out hunting for hake when a Norwegian coastguard boat announces that officers are coming aboard to inspect it

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The story of a tight-knit community of ship builders and factory workers in Clydebank, West Dunbartonshire, and how their lives were changed by the bombings of March 1941. The documentary focuses on the after-effects of one particular explosive, which destroyed number 78 Jellicoe Street, killing 15 members of the Rocks family. Surviving relatives and friends recall how they were deeply affected by the events, and how the return of the bombers led to a mass exodus from the town

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Irish resort of Skerries, the crew run to the station when strong winds sweep a teenager on a paddleboard towards the busy shipping lanes of the Irish Sea, while in the Bristol Channel, among the many yachts taking advantage of a fresh summer breeze, one loses a crew member overboard. While the crowds are heading home at the end of a busy summer's day in Cleethorpes, two 13-year-old girls find themselves alone, cut off and at risk of drowning on a sandbank rapidly disappearing under a fast-rising tide

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: David Jenkins, a world-leading surgeon at Royal Papworth, performs one of the most demanding operations possible, draining a patient's body of blood to remove clots in her lungs. At Addenbrooke's hospital, consultant orthopaedic surgeon Andrew Carrothers is also pushing at the boundaries of medical science with a complex operation that he has devised called the Harrington Plus. Frustrated by the lack of options for patients whose pelvis and hip bones have been badly weakened by cancer, Andrew added a crucial stage to an existing operation to enable cancer patients to walk again

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The World's War: Forgotten Soldiers of Empire
programme.desc: Part one of two. Historian and film-maker David Olusoga tells the story of First World War from the perspective of those of who made it a truly global conflict - the millions of African and Asian troops and ancillaries who served alongside Europeans. He begins by examining how the British and the French drew immediately on the military resources of their respective empires at the outbreak of the conflict, with Sikhs, Gurkhas, Garhwalis and Pathans from the Raj and Spahis, Zouaves and Senegalese from north and west Africa being among the first to experience the horrors of trench warfare

programme.title: The World's War: Forgotten Soldiers of Empire
programme.desc: Part two of two. Historian and film-maker David Olusoga explores how Germany enlisted Muslims from north Africa and the Middle East to join the fight against Britain and its allies during the First World War. Muslim PoWs and deserters held in special camps in Germany were indoctrinated to see Kaiser Wilhelm II as a lover of Islam and a supporter of national independence wherever the British or French flags flew. General Paul von Lettow-Vorbeck led an army of east African troops in an insurgency that cost the lives of hundreds of thousands of black troops

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Highlights of the past two series of the motoring show, including the time the team took a Yorkshire road trip in the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche. Plus, Paddy McGuinness building his own luxurious off-roader from scratch and Chris Harris putting the Rolls-Royce Cullinan through its paces

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and a couple of gold plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Two life-changing assaults cost a police officer his career and have a huge impact on his health as he is diagnosed with complex PTSD

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Weekly magazine for the deaf and hard of hearing. With voiceover

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of a family-run roadside recovery business in the Scottish Highlands, as they rescue stranded motorists on some of the most remote roads in Britain

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Croydon, south London, as chef Jonathan Jones and hospitality consultant Richard Arman use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After George finds out about Shay's relationship with Ike and their vandalism of local property, there is conflict in the Turner household

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The semi-final sees the remaining four contestants face three challenges testing their knowledge of complex patterns and cutting out. First they have to make an asymmetric yoked skirt featuring a fiendishly difficult curved seam, before they are asked to transform duvet cover into women's garments, and end by creating, constructing and fitting dresses of their own design. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young then decide which one leaves the competition and misses out on the final. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Michael Baggott assess items at Barrow Hill Roundhouse in Derbyshire. Presenter Paul Martin visits nearby Renishaw Hall to learn about the Sitwell Trio - three siblings who took the artistic and literary world by storm in the early 20th century

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: The Peterhead boat Amity is on its way to a fishing ground near the Shetland Isles when something gets tangled in its propeller, so skipper Phil Reid puts a waterproof camera over the side to try to work out what's causing the problem. A 300-ton trawler comes into Peterhead port for a paint job, with the workers having just six days to get it cleaned, painted and back into the water

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the deadly impact of an incendiary bomb that set fire to St Peter's Church in the historic heart of Bristol on the night of Sunday November 24, 1940. Almost 80 years later, Geoffrey Serle, who had been attending church with his father when the raid began, recounts their desperate attempt to find shelter amongst the chaos. The film also uses accounts from the time, recalling how Bristol's firefighters simply could not cope

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary heads to the Yorkshire coast to celebrate a unique bonfire party, explore the ancient seaport of Whitby and sample some classic, coastal comfort food - fish and chips and ice-cream. Mary visits a small smokehouse that has been run by the same family for five generations, where she helps smoke herring to make their world-renowned kippers. To end her time in Yorkshire, Mary helps a local community with their unique bonfire for Guy Fawkes Night. Inspired by all she sees, Mary makes a rustic smoked trout pate, followed by a one-pot haddock and shrimp feast

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheat's bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Return of the documentary that goes behind the scenes on the most complex murder and large-scale criminal investigations in Scotland. The detectives investigate the case of 37-year-old Paul Mathieson, who was assaulted and found fighting for his life on a street in Renfrew. The victim of a brutal assault, Paul succumbs to his horrific injuries, and the investigators of Police Scotland's Major Investigations Team take up the case

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Kevin reluctantly hosts a last-minute Halloween party, while Julia takes the kids trick-or-treating in an attempt to meet a celebrity who has just moved into the neighbourhood. Liz tags along as she has been stood up by a date and Anne is in attendance trying to make sure that the kids are safe - but by the time they reach Kevin's house one of the children is missing

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles and Monica arrive at Royal Mansour, one of the world's most discreet hotels. In stark contrast to the developing country it inhabits, the jewel of the city was built, with a limitless budget, by royal decree to showcase the kingdom to world leaders and to billionaire and celebrity guests

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Documentary following the stories of ordinary people fighting for truth and justice following acts of terror. The first one featured is a hostage crisis in Syria that culminated in seven beheadings by a British executioner, later dubbed Jihadi John, while the second looks at the far-right attack in Christchurch, New Zealand, in which 51 people were shot dead in the city's mosques

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Weekly magazine for the deaf and hard of hearing. With voiceover

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the Wirral coast, the crew of Hoylake's rescue hovercraft is called out to an unconventional casualty - a porpoise stranded by the outgoing tide. Two hundred miles to the north another unusual victim of the British tides, a teenage wedding guest in suit and tie, is plucked off a fast-disappearing rocky outcrop in the middle of the sea by the Kinghorn crew, while in Cornwall, the Penlee lifeboat crew receives a dreaded call - a fishing boat, skippered by a fisherman the crew all know, is in flames miles out at sea

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to diagnose two patients with mysterious symptoms that have baffled other doctors. Carys has endured a constant cycle of illness for the last nine years. She suffers with headaches, nausea and vomiting, repeated infections and a constant fever, including one occasion where she simultaneously had ear, sinus throat, chest and urine infections. While the experts begin their detective work on Carys's case, Michael introduces them to 43-year-old army veteran Andrew, who suffers up to 50 unexplained seizures every week, which is having a devastating effect on his life

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a recently retired couple find a dream country home in the counties of Shropshire and Herefordshire, and also visits the Weston Park Model Air Show

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A police officer shot at close range is in danger of losing his sight, and a speeding motorcyclist is pursued through London

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers prepare for the worst of the winter weather, and disaster strikes for Helen and Graham when one of their Highland cows contracts a chronic, contagious wasting disease. In the Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare for a sheepdog trial, while in Cumbria, Frank and his daughter Georgia acquire a small herd of pygmy goats. Joyce travels to Edinburgh to give a speech to mark the launch of the Women in Agriculture report at the Royal Highland and Agriculture headquarters

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: An antiques expert, a market trader and an upcycler try to make the most money for BBC Children in Need, beginning with Melissa Downhill, Kevin Paxton and Arusha Irvine

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George struggles to cope after Shay leaves. Meanwhile, the whole of Weld camp out on Big Mac's beach side field to protest his plans to build on it

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The competition reaches its climax as the three finalists enter the sewing room for the last time, with Esme Young and Patrick Grant setting them three challenges based on evening wear. They must first demonstrate their precision by creating a man's pin-tucked dress shirt and then have to transform a tuxedo into a little black dress in the alteration task. Finally they are asked to produce a floor-length evening gown for a model of their choice, before the judges decide which of them wins the title. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota in the summer to catch up with the black bears, but is upset to discover that cub Hope has been abandoned by her mother. With the young animal facing starvation, he is forced to step in and become a surrogate parent, and balances his new duties with continued study of the rest of the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Harper are on hand at the Watts Gallery in Surrey to find a selection of objects to take to auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: The fishing boat Reliance spends the day in port, two of its Ghanaian crewmen take a trip into Peterhead to buy gifts for their families back home, while on the Amity, skipper Phil Reid is trying a new fishing ground to see if he can find a decent catch

programme.title: The River Wye with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and angler joins a group of young people from across Britain as they take to the water at Glasbury, Powys, to travel 100 miles downriver by canoe and kayak, before he heads to Hay-on-Wye to learn how it reinvented itself as a literary hotspot, now famous for its festival

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary analyses the worlds of architecture and fashion during the pandemic, noting that while the clothes people wear have already changed, cities and homes need to change to accommodate new ways of living. She also compares the new normal with life in ancient Rome, and fulfils a 40-year ambition by reuniting three cast members of 1970s BBC drama I, Claudius - Derek Jacobi, Sian Phillips and Brian Blessed

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cherry Bakewell factory in Stoke on Trent that produces 250,000 tarts every day, and meets the team of 12 employed to precisely place the cherries on top by hand. Cherry Healey learns the tricks of avoiding a soggy pastry bottom when baking at home, and visits a factory where almonds are roasted and milled to make butter. Historian Ruth Goodman discovers the origins of frangipane as a perfume in 17th-century Paris, and discovers how the modern cherry Bakewell recipe was arrived at by accident

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: Police Scotland's major investigation teams deal with around 60 murders a year and this edition delves deeper into the nature of the job. Through reconstructions and interviews with both senior and rookie detectives, the film reveals what it is like to work on such devastating cases as the murder of Alesha MacPhail and the disappearance of Julie Reilly.

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian dissects the week's news with the help of guest panellists Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Sara Pascoe and Sophie Duker

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: A filmic offshoot of Tim Key's stage show Megadate, in which a man dazzles a woman on the perfect first date. However, she doesn't text him back, leading him to retrace his steps. His path is riddled with cynics and naysayers, questioning his optimistic outlook on how the date went. The nerd in the bowling alley who thought he'd embarrassed himself, the old eel catcher on the mud flats trying to douse his dreams with wisdom, and the compassionate waitress at the chicken shop. Starring Tim Key, Mandeep Dhillon, Sam Fletcher, Bill Paterson and Julie Dray

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: David Jenkins, a world-leading surgeon at Royal Papworth, performs one of the most demanding operations possible, draining a patient's body of blood to remove clots in her lungs. At Addenbrooke's hospital, consultant orthopaedic surgeon Andrew Carrothers is also pushing at the boundaries of medical science with a complex operation that he has devised called the Harrington Plus. Frustrated by the lack of options for patients whose pelvis and hip bones have been badly weakened by cancer, Andrew added a crucial stage to an existing operation to enable cancer patients to walk again

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian recounts the story of how the Romantics - fuelled by the failures of the French Revolution and horrors of the Industrial Revolution - retreated from the tragic uproar of public life to take a journey into their own minds. Here, long before Freud and Jung and the modern psychological revolution, they discovered the subconscious - something that would have a profound effect on the modern world. Contributors include Tobias Menzies, Peter Doherty, Piotr Anderszewski and David Attenborough

programme.title: Extinction: The Facts
programme.desc: With a million species at threat, David Attenborough explores the science of extinction and how this has consequences for everyone. The programme examines why biodiversity loss is happening and what it means - not just for the natural world, but for all mankind. The loss of biodiversity threatens food and water security, undermines humans' ability to control the climate, and places humans at greater risk of pandemics. Leading experts including Sir Robert Watson and plant scientist Prof Kathy Willis explain how all groups in the natural world are in decline, and that extinction is now happening up to 100 times faster than the natural evolutionary rate

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: Troy Scott continues to struggle with his task of maintaining one of the largest rhododendron collections in the country. However, Bodnant Garden faces greater trouble as reports arrive about a fast-spreading oak disease

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a beautiful summer scene - a rushing little riverlet flowing through the luscious greenery of a cabin landscape

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: A journey along the American river, whose fingers stretch into nearly half of the USA, collecting water from 31 states. The documentary follows the Mississippi through varied landscapes from its source to its mouth, starting in the towering Rocky Mountains of Wyoming and Montana, where billions of tons of water, ultimately destined to flow south are trapped as ice and snow

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer meets members of one of the most notorious and feared motorcycle gangs in Gold Coast, before heading to the suburbs to meet a vet and his team rescuing and treating injured koalas. In Sydney, he chats to a billionaire who made his fortune in housing for new migrants from Asia and visits the country's first Muslim women's Australian rules football team. On the last leg of his journey, Simon takes to the air to witness devastating and deadly fires ripping through the Bush, before celebrating Australia Day at his final destination of Melbourne

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: The first of four profiles marking Black History Month. Ellen E Jones narrates an exploration of the Get Out writer and director's career as a comedian, film-maker and producer in movies and TV, looking at how a lifelong love for the horror genre has informed his style

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: Corrina Antrobus explores the work of the film director, who was the first black woman to win Best Director at Sundance, for her romantic drama Middle of Nowhere

programme.title: TV's Black Renaissance: Reggie Yates in Hollywood
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how a wave of hit shows written by and starring African-American talent is shaking up the TV industry. Reggie Yates travels to LA to meet some of the stars, writers and directors of shows such as Atlanta, Dear White People and Insecure, including Mahershala Ali, Caleb McLaughlin, Lena Waithe and Justin Simien, and tackles some of the questions in Trump's America that their work addresses

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Mary is in peril as she is being used as bait, but Jay struggles to keep her safe as the threat closes in on both of them. Fran feels betrayed as she digs deeper into Zoe's disappearance

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Jay is lured into a trap when he tries to rescue Mary and an epic gun battle erupts, while Fran confronts the locals who have been implicated in past crimes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a beautiful summer scene - a rushing little riverlet flowing through the luscious greenery of a cabin landscape

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: Troy Scott continues to struggle with his task of maintaining one of the largest rhododendron collections in the country. However, Bodnant Garden faces greater trouble as reports arrive about a fast-spreading oak disease

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer meets members of one of the most notorious and feared motorcycle gangs in Gold Coast, before heading to the suburbs to meet a vet and his team rescuing and treating injured koalas. In Sydney, he chats to a billionaire who made his fortune in housing for new migrants from Asia and visits the country's first Muslim women's Australian rules football team. On the last leg of his journey, Simon takes to the air to witness devastating and deadly fires ripping through the Bush, before celebrating Australia Day at his final destination of Melbourne

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: Ann Smith implements an ambitious calendar of summer events to attract more visitors to Bodnant Garden. However, head gardener Troy Scott Smith becomes concerned an influx of crowds could ruin his immaculately prepared grounds

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross captures the graceful and uncommon qualities of distant sloping hills and a peaceful nearby lake

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts the stories of Britain's castles and their roles in the country's history, art and literature. He begins by looking at the structure's first appearance during Norman rule in 1066, and then at the siege of Kenilworth Castle 200 years later, revealing how it was initially used as an instrument of invasion, but soon became a weapon with which unruly barons challenged the crown. He also visits Tintagel Castle in Cornwall, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived

programme.title: The Secret Life of Landfill: A Rubbish History
programme.desc: In a unique science experiment, Dr George McGavin and Dr Zoe Laughlin are given unprecedented access to one of the UK's largest landfill sites to investigate the history of rubbish

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow embarks on a grime-filled journey through the histories of three of the world's leading modern cities. He begins with London, using CGI footage and re-enactments to experience life in the capital in the 14th century. He spends a night as a medieval muck-raker, and tries on a pair of wooden platform shoes designed to elevate the wearer above the filth that covered the streets

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: Lydia Wilson looks at the increasing use of the Latin alphabet, which in some languages means more than changing letters as it entails a change of identity.

programme.title: Searching for Sam: Adrian Dunbar on Samuel Beckett
programme.desc: Thirty years after the playwright's death in Paris at the age of 83, Line of Duty star Adrian Dunbar explores the life and work of Samuel Beckett to find out about the man behind Waiting for Godot. Although born and raised in Dublin, Ireland, he was a resident of Paris for most of his adult life, writing in both French and English, and was awarded the 1969 Nobel Prize in Literature for his work

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross captures the graceful and uncommon qualities of distant sloping hills and a peaceful nearby lake

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: Ann Smith implements an ambitious calendar of summer events to attract more visitors to Bodnant Garden. However, head gardener Troy Scott Smith becomes concerned an influx of crowds could ruin his immaculately prepared grounds

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts the stories of Britain's castles and their roles in the country's history, art and literature. He also visits Tintagel Castle in Cornwall, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: With Bodnant lying under a blanket of snow, head gardener Troy Scott Smith plans a winter-themed display to attract more visitors. Plus, a 300-year-old oak succumbs to age, and moles wreak havoc on the team's preparations for the new season

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lost little cabin and an ice-covered lake surrounded by a snowy mountain range

programme.title: The Searchers (1956)
programme.desc: An embittered American Civil War veteran sets out to find his niece, who has been abducted by a renegade Comanche tribe. His search takes him many years, and during the long quest his travelling companion starts to wonder if he intends to rescue her or kill her. John Ford's Western, starring John Wayne, Natalie Wood, Jeffrey Hunter and Vera Miles

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The adventurer explores how the American wilderness shaped the Wild West. He begins by looking at the landscapes of three great mountain ranges - the Appalachians, the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada - and how they challenged the westward push of early pioneers. He discovers how their geography, extreme weather, wild animals and ecology provided both opportunities and challenges for those living there, and also experiences mule-trekking and gold prospecting

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The creation of the band's second album, which took the gothic synth-pop foundations of the band and combined them with arena-ready anthems leading to critical acclaim and the international hit singles Mothers Talk, Shout and Everybody Wants to Rule the World. With full co-operation of the band members, this documentary explores how the album was recorded, leaving its indelible imprint on New Wave

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How variations on the theme of family are depicted in Australian movie-making The Castle's nuclear, if unorthodox family, a family of faith in The Devil's Playground, Romper Stomper's frightening Neo-Nazis and crime families such as those depicted in Ned Kelly and Animal Kingdom

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a lost little cabin and an ice-covered lake surrounded by a snowy mountain range

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.desc: Love Monster heads out to meet up with Book Cub, who has a special Super Sound Day surprise for him. Before he meets her, he hears a buzzing bee and has fun beatboxing to it.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies’ very own motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that help make roads! Grace helps operate a road planer, checks out a road printing machine and then has a go in a road paver!

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back in time to the era of the ammonites, which inhabited Earth 149 million years ago. They were a type of mollusc that lived in shoals in the sea. Andy is challenged to find a shell to replace the one he broke at the museum, and along the way he comes face to face with the eustreptospondylus - a swimming dinosaur

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: The opening of Purl's ice-cream parlour is at risk when the ice-cream doesn't arrive

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grandmaster Glitch makes popcorn in Mount Etna, Italy, he blocks the volcano and endangers farms on its slopes

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle feels left out of the Brooklyn-Bells' fun, so he sets off to find his doggy family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran go on a day trip to the seaside to build sandcastles!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Music Lady needs Sarah and Duck to help her return a music-loving stowaway back to the Cloud Tower

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Each of the friends decide to give Lily Plant some pretend water'

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The creatures are visited by a rare, strange and beautiful creature a Moon Whale. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Tombliboo Ooo makes himself ill by drinking too much Pinky Ponk juice, so his friends play music to make him feel better

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Ben Bailey Smith reads My Big Shouting Day, by Rebecca Patterson

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers visit a London bar with unpaid debts concerning work on the air conditioning, and a classic car dealer - but the man they meet at the car lot claims to have just taken over the premises. Plus, the case of a cleaner who took her employer to an employment tribunal over unpaid sick and holiday pay

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Three weeks on, the residents each face decisions about their future. Gail tries to remain dedicated to her marriage, while Belle finds raising Maya has an impact on her love life. David meets Kelly's university friends, and is left uncertain how well he knows her, while a chance encounter sees him reunited with Saira. Andy has a tense altercation with Liam that forces him to consider what he really wants out of life

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Simi and Prescillia test their commitment before deciding whether to move in together

programme.title: Just One Night

programme.title: Ambulance

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history behind some of the country's most popular spices and explore how they have been used to liven up meals. The culinary duo also visit a chilli festival - with mouth-numbing consequences

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton visits rural Shropshire with a couple who want their £500,000 budget to afford them and their young children a life in the outdoors with community close by. While in the county, Alistair visits the mansion of Attingham Park to see the recent restoration successes for himself, as well as discovering some intriguing stories from its past

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in York, helping a couple who have spent two years painstakingly renovating their terraced home. With a very small garden full of crazy paving and building materials, Charlie and the boys compete to give it a £5,000 facelift, turning it into a luxurious haven to relax and entertain in. Along the way, they have a host of ideas and tips for making the most of small spaces and shady corners

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a pocket-sized racing car, a century-old weather vane, and a much loved but horribly damaged leather rhinoceros

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike hit a new obstacle to nailing down the next event booking when the Captain's past threatens to be unearthed, and he wildly protests at the couple's endeavours. Meanwhile, Thomas and Julian battle it out over how the ghosts will spend their evening, Lady Button is confused by urges she hasn't felt before, and an old friend of Kitty's reappears

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. The team set to work repairing a Jamaican pump organ, an RAF bomber pilot's hat, a naval rigging tool kit, and a crib made from wood salvaged from a church that was bombed during the First World War

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Thirty minutes into the night shift, police request urgent assistance at the scene of an incident - a man has called to say his mum has been stabbed. On arrival paramedics find she is in cardiac arrest, and she is declared the sickest patient in London by Ambulance Control. Across town, Josh and Vinny are dealing with more practical issues. They've been dispatched to their first patient of the night - a woman who's collapsed in a casino. But to reach her, they need to first navigate Leicester Square, which is currently teeming with revellers. Narrated by Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Darby runs into an old flame and spends the rest of the day trying to get him off her mind. Romantic comedy anthology series, starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Feeling empowered after her experience with Danny, Darby embarks on a non-committal hook-up bender - until she falls for up-and-coming chef Magnus

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Checking into the Shankly this week is a party of 41 stags, who are being looked after by trainee guest relations manager James. But the morning before the lads arrive, he is nowhere to be found. Owner Lawrence challenges staff to set up a glamping festival on the roof in just one week, a loved-up couple need a last-minute bridal suite and a bride-to-be brings her friends to the hotel for her second hen party this year

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The quarantine continues and the supplies start getting low, and Prem is no wiser about the source of the virus. Megan and Emlyn come up with a plan to get more food from the next village, but events take a turn when Mary arrives with terrible news

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the British tradition of cooking with food hunted from the land and prepare a selection of dishes using rabbit, grouse and venison

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple looking for a property in Gloucestershire on a budget of £850,000 and visits an ice cream-making family on Rodburgh Common

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Oxfordshire mum Sarah is after a space where she can relax and unwind after a full-on day minding two young children, but is also aware that the garden also needs to work as a play space for the youngsters. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Bicester to assist Sarah and partner Adam, who need a garden the whole family can play and relax in

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team tackle a shattered ceramic dish, a hand-made doll's house and a vintage harmonium that provided the soundtrack to many an East End knees-up

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Chantelle's funeral arrives, and as family and friends gather to say goodbye, Gray struggles to hold it together. Whitney is about to face her first day in court for the death of Leo, with Sonia there for support - but neither of them are prepared for what happens next. Phil visits Raymond in hospital, feeling an instant bond with the boy, while Jean warns Kush about the risks of online gambling

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: A busy Friday sees almost 700 party-mad people checking in. But with just minutes to go, there are problems with the plumbing. It's even worse for Charley, who has arrived for a hen party organised by her brother, as there are problems with their room - and general manager Damian struggles to placate them. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the celebrity specials, which saw famous faces swapping showbusiness for big business to raise more than £2million for charity. Highlights include Karren Brady's debut on the show, heading up the women's team in the first celebrity special back in 2007, Jack Dee and Jonathan Ross attempting to design toys, and the moment Omid Djalili lost a carefully placed balloon - not to mention his dignity - as part of Rylan Clark-Neal's fundraising cabaret show

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason present the weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz
